assumptions: Use the board as a proof vehicle, not as the architecture limit.
Try the most aggressive candidate first, then fall back only if timing or fit fails.
Keep the AXI Stream/CSR architecture stable even if the internal backend is smaller.

candidates.name: tn9k_axis128_8rpc_single_context
notes: Use if timing and LUT utilization pass.
Good stress test for the small device.
candidates.summary: Aggressive Tang Nano 9K candidate: 128-bit AXIS, p8 in 1 cycle, p12 in 2 cycles.

candidates.name: tn9k_axis128_4rpc_single_context
notes: Recommended fallback if 8RPC fails timing or fit.
candidates.summary: Safer high-throughput candidate: 128-bit AXIS and 4 rounds per cycle.

candidates.name: tn9k_axis128_1rpc_known_good
notes: Use for debugging firmware/AXIS integration, not as the final FPGA architecture.
candidates.summary: Known-good fallback preserving the same interface with one round per cycle.
